segments.title: OPVs are maritime-security ships with wartime limits
segments.summary: The episode argues that OPVs are valuable maritime-security hulls, but limited warships unless designed and armed for wartime from the start.
segments.body: OPVs suit exclusive-economic-zone patrol, fisheries enforcement, border security, and forward presence, but upgrades in wartime run into weapons, crew, survivability, yard capacity, and industrial-base constraints. The Singaporean Independence class is used as the contrast because its wartime role is built into the design rather than assumed as a later refit.

evidence.note: The opening defines OPVs as peacetime frontline vessels for exclusive-economic-zone patrol and interdiction rather than high-end combatants.
The River-class discussion contrasts maritime-security usefulness with limited weapons, survivability, and anti-submarine design.
The wartime step-up section warns that weapon stockpiles, shipyard capacity, and industrial stress tests make rapid conversion assumptions fragile.
The finale uses Singapore's Independence class and the high-low maritime-security balance to frame when OPVs need built-in wartime capability.
